name: George Masso
abstract: George Masso (November 17, 1926 – October 22, 2019) was an American jazz trombonist, bandleader, vibraphonist, and composer specializing in swing and Dixieland. Masso is notable for his work from 1948 to 1950 as a member of the Jimmy Dorsey band.

interviews.title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other
Kool Jazz Festival: Stormy Weather: A Salute to Ethel Waters
JVC Jazz Festival: For the Love of Louis

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: I played with George. And I really enjoyed it, you know. It was fun.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been over since then too with a lot of the same guys, Kenny Davern and Yank Lawson and George Masso, and Ralph Sutton, and Jake Hanna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: So he had me get the band, and I had Jake and Bud Johnson and George Masso, Cassius Fields was out there.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: he played, I played and we had a wonderful time... when we were on the plane coming back from Galveston
